sense.definition: a game played on a table with two players, in which the players use special sticks to hit 16 numbered balls into the holes at the edge of the table

examples.x: Pool is a popular recreational activity enjoyed by people of all ages around the world.
Playing pool requires skill, strategy, and precision to accurately hit the balls into the pockets.
Pool tournaments attract skilled players who compete for prizes and prestige in various formats of the game.
The sound of balls clacking against each other and the smooth glide of the cue stick on the felt adds to the ambiance of a pool hall.

sense.definition: a game in which players use a cue stick, aiming to sink all their balls before pocketing the black eight ball to win

examples.x: In eight ball, the player must call their shot before making it.
His strategy in eight ball involves careful ball placement.
She enjoys playing eight ball with friends at the local bar.
They organized an eight ball tournament at the pool hall.
Eight ball is popular for its strategic and competitive play.

sense.definition: a cue sport played with nine numbered balls and a cue ball, where the objective is to pocket the 9-ball

examples.x: His favorite pool game has always been nine ball.
The tournament featured some of the best nine ball players in the world.
In nine ball, players must hit the lowest numbered ball first.
The nine ball rolled slowly into the corner pocket.

sense.definition: a type of cue sport game where each player is assigned one specific pocket and must only score balls into that pocket

examples.x: In one-pocket, each player must carefully plan their moves.
The one-pocket tournament drew skilled players from across the country.
The one-pocket championship was the highlight of the pool hall's events.
One-pocket requires a deep understanding of angles and positioning.
The one-pocket match lasted for hours, full of suspense and strategy.

sense.definition: a variation of pool played on a special green table with 1 white ball, 15 red balls, and 6 balls of other colors
a table game in which two players compete by trying to direct balls into holes around the table using special long sticks called cues

examples.x: I played billiards with my friends at the local club last night.
He’s really good at billiards, especially when it comes to making tricky caroms.
After dinner, we decided to play a few rounds of billiards to pass the time.
I’ve never tried billiards before, but it looks like a fun game to learn.
We spent the whole afternoon practicing our billiards skills for the tournament.

sense.definition: a cue sport in which players score points by bouncing the cue ball off two other balls

examples.x: She practiced carom billiards daily to perfect her technique.
The carom billiards tournament attracted players from all over the world.
The carom billiards match was intense, with both players displaying remarkable skill.
They set up a carom billiards table in the club’s game room.
Carom billiards requires a deep understanding of geometry and strategy.

sense.definition: a traditional pub game played on a small table with pockets, combining elements of billiards and bagatelle, providing a social and skill-based pastime in pubs and bars

examples.x: We played bar billiards at the pub last night, and I almost won.
Bar billiards is such a fun game, it’s perfect for a casual evening with friends.
He is really good at bar billiards, always scoring high points.
She spent hours at the pub playing bar billiards with her friends.
After a few rounds of bar billiards, we decided to grab a drink and relax.

sense.definition: a cue sport where the player must hit the cue ball to contact at least three cushions before striking the second object ball

examples.x: Three-cushion billiards is popular in many European countries.
She explained the rules of three-cushion billiards to her friends.
Three-cushion billiards can be challenging for beginners.
The tournament featured some of the best three-cushion billiards players.
Three-cushion billiards requires precise calculation and skill.

sense.definition: the act of successfully hitting a ball into a pocket in pool and snooker

examples.x: Her skillful pot of the black ball clinched the victory.
The player’s third consecutive pot demonstrated his exceptional talent.
Her series of pots in quick succession amazed everyone watching.
The crowd erupted in cheers after his stunning pot of the final ball.
His flawless pot of the red ball drew applause from the spectators.

sense.definition: a strategic play where the player aims to leave the opponent with a difficult or low-scoring shot

examples.x: He played a safety shot, tucking the cue ball behind the red.
After a risky miss, she opted for a safety shot to regain control.
His safety shot left the opponent with no clear path to the pocket.
The safety shot positioned the cue ball near the cushion.
She executed a perfect safety shot, making it hard for the other player.

sense.definition: a shot in cue sports where the ball is bounced off the cushion before hitting the target

examples.x: He made a perfect bank shot to sink the eight ball.
The game-winning bank shot left the audience in awe.
She practiced her bank shot for hours to improve her accuracy.
His bank shot strategy always surprises his opponents.
A tricky bank shot secured his victory in the tournament.

sense.definition: a shot where the cue ball is intentionally jumped over an obstacle ball to pocket another ball or avoid a foul

examples.x: The cue ball bounced over the cluster of balls in a stunning jump shot.
With skillful cue action, she managed a precise jump shot to pocket the red ball.
The jump shot was risky but necessary to reach the last remaining ball on the table.
After studying the angles, he attempted a jump shot to pocket the ball near the cushion.
His jump shot was applauded for its accuracy and finesse.

sense.definition: (cue sports) the situation where the cue ball bounces off the table cushions before hitting another ball

examples.x: He celebrated his victory with a signature cushion shot.
The cushion shot deflected unexpectedly, leaving a tough follow-up shot.
She struggled with the cushion shot, overshooting the intended position.
His cushion shot technique was flawless, impressing spectators.
The cushion shot angle was difficult, but he managed to sink the ball.

sense.definition: the act of hitting one ball with the cue ball, which then strikes one or more other balls on the table without any balls being pocketed into a pocket

examples.x: He executed a perfect carom, bouncing off two rails to hit the target ball.
Caroms require precise angle and speed calculations.
They studied the physics of caroms to understand rebound angles.
His carom left the opponent with a difficult position.
The carom was crucial in breaking up a cluster of balls on the table.

sense.definition: a surprising piece of good luck

examples.x: Winning the lottery was a complete fluke for him.
She passed the difficult exam by a fluke.
Finding the rare coin in the attic was a fluke.
The sunny weather on their wedding day was a fluke considering the forecast.

sense.definition: (cue sports) a shot where the cue ball is hit with extreme spin, causing it to curve sharply around obstacles

examples.x: The masse shot is considered one of the most challenging shots in pool.
He executed a perfect masse shot to curve the cue ball around the obstructing ball.
She practiced the masse shot tirelessly until she mastered it.
The angle for the masse shot was difficult, but he managed to pocket the ball.
She studied the mechanics of the masse shot to improve her game.

sense.definition: a shot in cue sports where the cue ball is struck below center to make it reverse its path after hitting an object ball

examples.x: His draw shot caused the cue ball to spin back towards him.
She executed a perfect draw shot to position for the next ball.
The player's draw shot stopped the cue ball dead for the easy next shot.
After practicing for hours, he finally mastered the draw shot.
He used a draw shot to avoid a cluster of balls and clear the table.

sense.definition: a situation in cue sports where the cue ball strikes two object balls successively in a single stroke

examples.x: He executed a perfect double shot, sinking both the 7-ball and the 8-ball in one stroke.
The double shot was called foul because the cue ball made contact with the 9-ball before the intended 6-ball.
In a surprising move, she attempted a double shot to clear the table, but missed the second ball.
The referee confirmed it was a legal double shot as the cue ball touched the object balls simultaneously.
His double shot left the 5-ball in a tough position for his opponent, setting up an easy win.

sense.definition: (cue sports) a shot where the cue ball is struck above center with enough force to follow the object ball into the pocket

examples.x: He nailed the force follow shot to sink the ball cleanly.
Her force follow shot set up an easy shot on the next ball.
They practiced force follow shots all afternoon.
The force follow shot requires precise control.
A good force follow shot requires a steady hand.

sense.definition: a shot in cue sports where the cue ball is struck directly towards the object ball without any spin

examples.x: His opponent missed the straight shot, giving him a chance to win the frame.
The player executed a flawless straight shot to pocket the red ball.
She practiced her straight shot to improve accuracy.
He lined up the straight shot on the 8-ball for an easy win.
With a confident stroke, he nailed the straight shot.

sense.definition: a shot in cue sports where one ball is driven into another to pocket a third ball

examples.x: He surprised everyone with an unexpected plant shot.
He executed a perfect plant shot to sink the 8-ball.
The plant shot set up an easy win for her team.
She lined up the plant shot to clear the table.
Practicing the plant shot improved his overall game strategy.

sense.definition: one of the six holes or openings on the table where balls are aimed to be legally sunk during gameplay in cue sports such as pool and billiards

examples.x: They marked the position of each pocket on the pool table.
The cue ball barely missed the pocket, leaving a difficult shot for the opponent.

sense.definition: the act of scoring three consecutive wins or points in cue sports
(snooker) a break of 100 points or more achieved in one visit to the table

examples.x: He scored his first century break in a crucial match.
She made a brilliant century to win the frame.
His highest century break is 147, achieved with a flawless run.
Achieving a century under pressure demonstrates mental strength and focus.
Players often aim to break the century mark to gain a significant lead.

sense.definition: (primarily in the game of billiards, snooker, or pool) to be the first player who strikes at the beginning of a game

examples.x: To start the match, the player who breaks must aim carefully.
In this round, I'll let you break and see how the balls scatter.
The first player to break often has an advantage in pool.

sense.definition: (cue sports) a foul where the cue tip remains in contact with the cue ball at the moment of cueing

examples.x: He was penalized for a push shot because his cue didn't cleanly strike the cue ball.
The push shot resulted in a loss of turn for the player.
The referee called a foul on the push shot as the player failed to execute a clean stroke.
The opponent was awarded four points due to a push shot by his competitor.
The player regretted the push shot that cost him the match.
